title: Which Tom Hanks Character Would You Actually Be In A Crisis?
dek: Would you fly the plane, quote a philosophy book on a raft, or just ask everyone to be your neighbor — take this quiz and find out.

quiz.intro: No wrong answers here — just answer like the disaster/dilemma is actually happening to you right now.

questions.question: The plane's engines just failed. What's your first move?

answers.text: Start talking to the stranger next to me about my life story
Try to make everyone laugh so nobody panics
Look for the nearest deserted island to make the best of it
Stay calm, memorize every exit, and start planning six moves ahead

questions.question: Someone on your team just messed up badly. How do you handle it?

answers.text: Crack a joke to break the tension immediately
Figure it out alone; I don't need anyone else to survive this
Stay firm but calm — de-escalate before anyone gets hurt
Treat it like a puzzle to solve, not a person to blame

questions.question: What's your love language when comforting a stranger?

answers.text: I talk to objects like they're my best friends
Direct honesty, even when it's uncomfortable
I explain the deeper historical meaning behind their pain
I tell them their story deserves to be told to the whole world

questions.question: Pick the item you'd want stranded with you on a desert island:

answers.text: A weapon and a plan for rescue
An ancient manuscript to decode
A notebook to sketch my next big idea
A working radio and a checklist

questions.question: The government/press turns on you after you did the right thing. What now?

answers.text: I dig into the evidence until I can prove everyone wrong
I charm my way back into everyone's good graces
I trust the facts will eventually clear my name
I stay gentle and patient, certain kindness wins eventually

questions.question: What's your ideal way to spend a Sunday afternoon?

answers.text: Pitching my next big dream to anyone who'll listen
Doing a quiet, methodical hobby that requires total focus
Changing into comfortable shoes and checking on my neighbors
Running — just running, no particular reason

questions.question: How do you deal with being called a hero for something you didn't feel like a hero doing?

answers.text: I deflect it — I was just doing my job with my training
I gently remind everyone it was a team effort, not just me
I shrug and say life's just a box of chocolates anyway
I make a joke so it doesn't feel like a big deal

questions.question: Pick a motto to live by:

answers.text: "Won't you be my neighbor?"
"Life is like a box of chocolates."
"There's a snake in my boot!"
"I have to make fire."

questions.question: How do you actually process grief or loss?

answers.text: I visit the place that reminds me of them and just talk
I distract myself by looking out for the people around me
I throw something meaningful into the sea and let it go
I hold it together for others, then break down once they're safe

questions.question: Someone asks you for advice on a huge, scary decision. What do you tell them?

answers.text: "Reach for the sky, but bring your friends along."
"Sometimes you just have to survive today and figure out tomorrow later."
"Stay calm, and don't let them see you scared."
"Look closer — the answer's probably hiding in plain sight."

results.name: Forrest Gump
results.subtitle: Forrest Gump (1994)
results.description: You're kind, loyal, and somehow always end up in the middle of history without trying — you just show up, do the next right thing, and let the chocolates fall where they may. People underestimate you constantly, and you keep proving them wrong by simply being decent.

results.name: Woody
results.subtitle: Toy Story (1995)
results.description: You're the loyal leader of your friend group, a little anxious about being replaced, but ultimately someone who'd do anything for the people (or toys) you love. You crack jokes to keep the mood light, even when you're panicking on the inside.

results.name: Chuck Noland
results.subtitle: Cast Away (2000)
results.description: You're resourceful, stubborn, and weirdly capable of finding meaning in total isolation — even if that meaning is a volleyball with a face drawn in blood. You'd rather figure it out alone than wait for rescue.

results.name: Captain Phillips
results.subtitle: Captain Phillips (2013)
results.description: You're steady under pressure and fiercely protective of your people, staying composed on the outside even as everything falls apart. You're the person everyone else looks to during the actual emergency, then you fall apart afterward, once it's safe.

results.name: Robert Langdon
results.subtitle: The Da Vinci Code (2006)
results.description: You're the one who overthinks everything — in a good way. You see patterns, symbols, and hidden meaning where everyone else sees a dead end, and you'd rather research your way out of a crisis than wing it.

results.name: Walt Disney
results.subtitle: Saving Mr. Banks (2013)
results.description: You're a dreamer who turns pain into something beautiful, using charm and persistence to win people over even when they've spent twenty years telling you no. You believe storytelling can heal things that facts alone can't.

results.name: Sully Sullenberger
results.subtitle: Sully (2016)
results.description: You're calm, prepared, and quietly heroic — the kind of person who does the impossible thing in seconds and then spends way longer second-guessing yourself about it afterward. You'd rather deflect the credit and share it with your whole team.

results.name: Mr. Rogers
results.subtitle: A Beautiful Day in the Neighborhood (2019)
results.description: You're gentle, patient, and genuinely curious about other people's feelings, even when the world around you is moving too fast for that kind of kindness. You believe slowing down and really listening is its own kind of quiet superpower.

sources.title: Tom Hanks on becoming Walt Disney in 'Saving Mr. Banks' | LAist
